meta.title: Security & Privacy
meta.description: How Vaulto protects your family's information — encryption in transit and at rest, hashed passwords, strict access control. Plain language, no overclaim.

hero.heading: Your family's information, protected.
hero.intro: Vaulto holds sensitive details — what you own, where it is, the documents that prove it. We treat that responsibility seriously. Here's exactly how we protect it, in plain language.

protections.title: Encrypted in transit.
protections.body: Every connection to Vaulto uses TLS (HTTPS), so your data is encrypted as it travels between you and us.

protections.title: Encrypted at rest.
protections.body: Your information and documents are stored on encrypted infrastructure — managed database and object storage.

protections.title: Passwords never stored plainly.
protections.body: We hash your password with a strong, modern algorithm (scrypt) — even we can't read it.

protections.title: Short-lived sessions.
protections.body: Sign-in tokens are short-lived and kept in memory; the token that keeps you signed in lives in a secure, script-proof cookie.

protections.title: Protected against abuse.
protections.body: We rate-limit sign-in and other sensitive actions to defend against automated attacks.

protections.title: Access controls.
protections.body: You can only see and edit your own vault. Our systems enforce this on every request.

promises.title: We don't share to advertise.
promises.body: Your information isn't handed to third parties to market to you.

promises.title: You're in control.
promises.body: You decide what you add — and what you remove.

yours.heading: Your data is yours
yours.body: Your data is yours — export everything (your data + your original files) at any time. From Settings, Download everything (.zip) gives you one file with a complete, machine-readable copy of your records plus every original photo and scan you uploaded. No lock-in, no waiting on us. The machine-readable export also satisfies your right to data portability under GDPR (Article 20).

honesty.body: Plain-language, no overclaim. We describe only what's true today: encryption in transit and at rest, hashed passwords, strict access control. We deliberately do not claim "zero-knowledge," "bank-level," or any certification we haven't earned. Data export is live (above). When we add app-layer encryption or a formal third-party audit, this page will say so.
honesty.contact: For privacy or security questions, contact
